Rotala 'Green'

Rotala rotundifolia 'Green'
DifficultyEasy
Growth RateFast
Light NeedsMedium - High
CO2Recommended
pH5.0 - 7.0
PositionBackground

✨ Why Ease-Aquatics Rotala?

Our Rotala Green is locally grown to stay vibrant and bushy. Unlike the standard rotundifolia, the 'Green' variety stays pure lime green even under high-intensity light, never turning orange or red. It is Pretoria-hardened for a seamless transition to your scape.

Advanced Technical Info

  • Growth Habit: Unlike other Rotalas, 'Green' tends to grow horizontally or weeping when first planted under high light before pushing upwards.
  • Color Stability: If your Rotala is turning red, it isn't the 'Green' variety. This specific strain is prized for maintaining its fresh spring-green look.
  • Nutrient Demand: A fast grower that loves nitrates. If leaves look pale or small, increase your liquid fertilization.
  • Shrimp Safe: Provides excellent cover for shrimp colonies once it forms a dense thicket.

Care & Maintenance

  • Pruning: ⚠️ Critical: To get the "bush" look, you must prune frequently. Cut the stems low; the plant will then produce multiple side-shoots from the cut point.
  • Light: Adapts to medium light, but high light creates much denser, more attractive leaf nodes.
  • CO2: Not strictly required, but CO2 injection will significantly increase growth speed and leaf size.

How to Propagate

  • Stem Cuttings: Simply snip a healthy stem (at least 10cm long) and remove the lower leaves.
  • Replanting: Push the bare stem into the substrate. It will develop a new root system within days.
  • Density: Plant stems about 1-2cm apart to allow light to reach the lower portions of the plant as it matures.

✅ Is this for you?

  • You want a vibrant, lime-green background bush.
  • You enjoy regular maintenance and scaping/trimming.
  • You need a fast-growing plant to help out-compete algae.
